content.thinking:
The `StepperContent` components are placed outside of the `<Stepper>` context provider, which means they won't have access to `useStepper()` and will throw errors. I need to either:

1. Move the `StepperContent` inside the `<Stepper>` component, or
2. Make `StepperContent` not use the context (just use the `step` prop and compare with `activeStep` passed directly)

Looking at the requirements again: `StepperContent` — "Only renders children when `step === activeStep` from context". So it uses context.


I'll restructure the demo to place all the content panels inside the `Stepper` component so they can properly access the context.

content.text:
Everything is in place. Here's a summary of what was created and modified: ## Created Files ### 1. `apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx` A compound component with 7 exports, following all shadcn/ui conventions: - **`Stepper`** — Root container using `React.createContext` to share `activeStep` and `orientation` with children. Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation`, and appropriate flex layout. - **`StepperItem`** — Wrapper per step with a nested `StepperItemContext` providing `step` and computed `state` (`"completed"` | `"active"` | `"pending"`). Renders `data-slot="stepper-item"`, `data-step`, and `data-state`. - **`StepperTrigger`** — Clickable `

Renders a `
` with `data-slot="stepper"`, `data-orientation`, and appropriate flex layout. - **`StepperItem`** — Wrapper per step with a nested `StepperItemContext` providing `step` and computed `state` (`"completed"` | `"active"` | `"pending"`).
